# UC-13.6.11 · Grafana LDAP, OAuth, and OIDC Team-Sync Failure Detection

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Intermediate &middot; **Pillar:** Platform &middot; **Type:** Availability, Configuration &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We watch the system that copies your company groups into dashboard teams. When that copy breaks, new people wait for access while old memberships linger. We flag those breaks early so admins fix directory links before work stops.*

---

## Description

Detects Grafana Enterprise team synchronization failures where LDAP, generic OAuth, or OIDC pipelines stop mapping directory groups to Grafana teams so new hires lose folder access and regulated orgs retain stale memberships. The signal is the sync mechanism itself—team_sync scheduler outcomes, auth.client_error fingerprints, discovery and JWKS errors, bind and TLS faults, schedule overruns, group coverage shortfalls versus inventory baselines, and per-node disagreement across HA members—not login success ratios (UC-13.6.5), not datasource query faults (UC-13.6.1 and UC-13.6.2), not alert evaluation drift (UC-13.6.3), not plugin skew (UC-13.6.4), not annotation storms (UC-13.6.6), not RBAC or permission ACL drift against static baselines (UC-13.6.10), and not notification delivery failures (UC-13.6.13).

## Value

Identity and observability leaders shorten IdP cutover incidents by correlating Grafana sync telemetry with Azure AD and Okta failures, quantifying coverage and latency regressions against a seven-day behavior band, and proving whether only one HA node lost directory context.

## Implementation

Ingest Grafana Enterprise server and audit logs with team_sync and auth.client_error lines into index=grafana, land Azure AD sign-in and Okta system logs via Splunk Add-on for Microsoft 365 and Okta inputs, publish grafana_idp_inventory.csv with expected_ad_group_count and sync_sla_seconds, accelerate CIM Authentication and Change for tstats arms, schedule observability_uc_13_6_11_grafana_team_sync_failures every ten to fifteen minutes over twenty-four hours, alert on critical or high severity rows, and route to platform identity on-call with IdP change correlation.

## Detailed Implementation

### Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform sponsors this control together with Grafana Enterprise administrators, directory engineers who own LDAP and Active Directory, cloud identity teams responsible for Azure AD app registrations and Okta authorization servers, and Splunk platform engineers who operate HTTP Event Collector or Universal Forwarder pipelines. This use case is intentionally narrow: it covers the scheduled and on-demand mechanisms that map external groups to Grafana teams, including Enterprise team_sync schedules, OAuth and OIDC client exchanges toward generic OAuth providers, and LDAP bind or search paths. It does not replace UC-13.6.5 login audit narratives focused on authentication success and failure ratios at the session layer. It does not replace UC-13.6.10 permission drift analytics that diff RBAC rows, folder ACLs, and inventory snapshots after users already authenticate. It stays distinct from UC-13.6.1 and UC-13.6.2 datasource query errors, UC-13.6.3 alert rule evaluation drift, UC-13.6.4 plugin catalog skew, UC-13.6.6 annotation storms, and UC-13.6.13 notification channel failures.

Operational prerequisites include time synchronization within a few seconds across Grafana HA members, LDAP load-balancers, OAuth clients, and Splunk indexers so invalid_grant and id_token_validation_failed patterns are not artifacts of skew. You need Grafana Enterprise licensing where team_sync is authoritative, or an equivalent automation job that emits comparable structured logs in OSS environments. Network paths from each Grafana replica to LDAPS, OAuth token endpoints, OIDC discovery documents, and JWKS URIs must be documented with expected latency envelopes. Identity teams must publish rotation calendars for client secrets, signing certificates, and LDAP service account passwords so Splunk analysts can correlate spikes with planned work.

Inventory discipline requires grafana_idp_inventory.csv with idp_tenant, expected_ad_group_count, sync_sla_seconds, ha_pool_name, and maint_window_tag columns. The expected_ad_group_count encodes how many directory groups should appear after a healthy sync for that tenant; Grafana logs that report a lower mapped count trigger coverage_gap logic. The sync_sla_seconds column sets milliseconds budgets compared against median sync_duration_ms in pipeline logs. HA pools list every Grafana hostname that should observe identical IdP outcomes; when only one member shows auth.client_error bursts, the ha_skew_hint flag guides failover reviews.

Risk briefing: IdP cutovers often run blue-green directory forests or parallel OAuth apps. Expect duplicate noise until cutover completes; gate pages with change tickets. Regulated environments may require retaining raw error traces under legal hold; scrub personal identifiers in downstream dashboards while keeping forensic copies in restricted indexes.

Differentiation recap: sync mechanism health, coverage, latency, HA symmetry, and cross-provider correlation—not login volume charts, not ACL diffs against static permission baselines, not panel query failures, not contact-point delivery traces.

### Step 2 — Configure data collection

Lane A, Grafana Enterprise server logs: enable structured logging at info or warn for authentication subsystems and team synchronization. Ship stdout, file, or systemd journal lines to index=grafana with sourcetype=grafana:log or sourcetype=grafana:server:log depending on your Splunk Add-on for Grafana mapping. Preserve logger names that emit auth.client_error, team_sync, ldap, oauth, and oidc keywords. Include host, instance, or pod identifiers on every row so HA skew detection can group by graf_host.

Lane B, Grafana audit trail: enable grafana:audit events for security-relevant actions and ensure HA members write a consistent schema. Forward audit JSON with action fields that reference teams, groups, ldap-sync, oauth, or oidc when available. This lane supplies per-node evidence when only one replica mis-parses directory attributes.

Lane C, Azure AD diagnostics: onboard Microsoft Entra ID sign-in logs using Splunk Add-on for Microsoft 365 or Azure Monitor streaming into sourcetype=azure:aad:signin or o365:audit:azureactivedirectory. Retain failure ResultType rows, error codes such as 50126 and 7000215, certificate-related errors after AD FS or app-proxy rotations, and client secret mismatches after app registration edits.

Lane D, Okta system logs: enable Okta Identity Engine or Classic system log ingestion with sourcetype=OktaIM2:log or your standardized okta sourcetype. Capture OAuth2, OIDC, and directory agent outcomes, invalid_grant, policy blocks, and key rotation events that align with Grafana client IDs used for team_sync.

Knowledge object: create lookups/grafana_idp_inventory.csv and grant search read access to the observability role. Optional companion grafana_team_baseline.csv can list team IDs and last-known membership counts for diffing, but the primary join for this search uses idp_tenant keys in grafana_idp_inventory.csv.

CIM alignment: map Azure AD, Okta, and Grafana authentication-adjacent events into the Authentication data model where field extractions populate action, src, dest, user, and app. Map Grafana configuration edits that touch auth settings into the Change data model with Change.dest equal to the Grafana hostname so tstats joins attach change_touch_count. Accelerate both models on indexers that serve this workspace.

Example validation queries after enabling inputs:

index=grafana sourcetype=grafana:log team_sync OR auth.client_error earliest=-1h
index=grafana sourcetype=grafana:audit team OR ldap earliest=-1h
index=o365 sourcetype=azure:aad:signin ResultType=failure earliest=-1h

Confirm volume stays within indexer capacity; team_sync can be chatty in large orgs, so consider filtering to warn and error severities at the forwarder when info floods indexes.

### Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL exactly as the top-level spl field in this JSON document under the saved search name observability_uc_13_6_11_grafana_team_sync_failures. Schedule every ten or fifteen minutes with dispatch.earliest_time=-24h@h and dispatch.latest_time=now for production. Alert when severity is critical or high after a deduplication window of ten minutes keyed on graf_host, sync_signature, and idp_provider. Include recommended_action, ha_skew_hint, coverage_gap, provider_window_errors, and cim_auth_failures in the email or incident payload so responders open the correct runbook without rerunning the entire multisearch during a bridge.

#### Understanding this SPL: multisearch fans four parallel lanes so a silent Grafana file descriptor issue cannot hide an Azure AD outage. The coalesce functions normalize host and tenant naming drift between Cloud, Kubernetes, and VM deployments. inputlookup inside join enriches each row with expected_ad_group_count and sync_sla_seconds so coverage_gap and latency tiers are policy-aware. streamstats rolling_lane_events highlights sudden bursts per provider signature inside the trailing window. eventstats medians summarize sync_duration_ms for Grafana pipeline logs without collapsing raw rows too early. The first join subsearch with tstats against Change counts configuration touches on each graf_host during the same day, helping distinguish secret rotations from pure network faults. The second join with tstats against Authentication counts enterprise-wide authentication failures as coarse context when IdP outages broaden beyond Grafana. case maps combinations of sync_signature, coverage_gap, ha_skew_hint, latency breaches, and error_trace substrings into severity. The closing table exposes seventeen analyst columns including error_trace for forensics.

Optional savedsearches.conf sketch:

[observability_uc_13_6_11_grafana_team_sync_failures_alert]
cron_schedule = */10 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -24h@h
dispatch.latest_time = now
enableSched = 1
action.email = 1
action.email.to = platform-identity-oncall@example.com
action.email.subject = Grafana team sync failure $result.severity$ host=$result.graf_host$ provider=$result.idp_provider$
counttype = number of events
relation = greater than
quantity = 0
search = | savedsearch observability_uc_13_6_11_grafana_team_sync_failures | where severity IN ("critical","high")

Performance guidance: if queue times exceed service targets, summarize each multisearch arm hourly into a summary index keyed on idp_provider, sync_signature, and graf_host, widen detection windows on summaries, and retain the full search for investigations only.

### Step 4 — Validate

Ground truth begins in Grafana Enterprise team settings and team_sync status screens for the same org and window as a Splunk row. Pick a critical severity event with sync_signature team_sync_job, open Grafana server logs on that graf_host, and confirm the directory bind or OAuth exchange failed with the same substring captured in error_trace. For Azure AD arms, locate the sign-in correlation ID in Entra ID portal diagnostics and verify error code and application ID match the Grafana enterprise application registration. For Okta arms, open the system log detail view and confirm client_id, grant type, and policy outcome align with the Splunk timestamp.

HA skew validation: when ha_skew_hint equals one, compare ldap.toml, oauth settings, and team_sync feature flags between cluster members. Export runtime settings via supported APIs and diff them; mismatched environment variables on a single pod commonly explain one-sided errors.

Coverage validation: when coverage_gap equals one, compare groups_mapped from Grafana structured logs against expected_ad_group_count in grafana_idp_inventory.csv. If inventory is stale after a directory restructure, refresh the CSV before treating the alert as a Grafana defect.

Latency validation: compare med_sync_ms against sync_sla_seconds multiplied by one thousand. If latency is high but errors are absent, investigate LDAP pagination or slow Okta group APIs rather than TLS failures.

Synthetic positive: in lab, revoke a test OAuth client secret while team_sync runs and confirm critical or high severity within two scheduled windows with oauth_token_exchange or azure_app_secret_rotation class signatures.

Synthetic negative: restore the secret, rerun sync, and confirm severities return to low for the test tenant across six consecutive runs.

### Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case A — Active Directory password rotation for the LDAP bind account invalidates TLS or simple-bind credentials mid-schedule: update the service account in Grafana ldap.toml, redeploy consistently across HA members, and replay a manual sync before closing the incident.

Case B — LDAP server TLS certificate expires or intermediate chain rotates during an infrastructure patch: renew certificates on directory load-balancers, refresh trust stores on Grafana hosts when mutual TLS applies, and validate with openssl s_client from each replica.

Case C — OAuth client_secret rotation without updating Grafana oauth configuration: rotate secrets in Azure AD or Okta, paste the new secret into Grafana generic OAuth settings atomically across replicas, and revoke superseded secrets only after health checks pass.

Case D — OIDC discovery endpoint returns intermittent 5xx from the IdP edge: fail over to secondary discovery URLs when the platform supports it, widen timeouts temporarily, and engage IdP vendor support with correlation IDs from auth.client_error lines.

Case E — Scheduled team_sync overruns its cron window after a directory import doubles group cardinality: increase sync frequency cautiously, raise LDAP search limits with directory owners, or shard teams across multiple Grafana orgs.

Case F — IdP group rename removes a mapped distinguished name so Grafana cannot resolve membership: update team external group IDs in Grafana, refresh grafana_idp_inventory.csv, and communicate naming standards to directory teams.

Case G — Very large organizations with hundreds of mapped groups hit LDAP query timeouts: implement paged results, narrow base DNs, or move to SCIM-style automation if Enterprise features allow.

Case H — team_sync jobs paused during a mistaken maintenance flag: re-enable sync in Grafana Enterprise settings, clear feature flags, and document the change ticket that authorized the pause.

Case I — HA config drift where node two lacks updated oauth client IDs: export configuration from the healthy member, reconcile Kubernetes ConfigMaps or Consul keys, and restart affected pods in a controlled order.

Case J — Clock skew between Grafana and IdP breaks id_token_validation_failed: synchronize NTP on all replicas and hypervisors, then replay sync.

Case K — Azure AD conditional access suddenly blocks the Grafana service principal during a policy rollout: add compliant device or IP conditions, or grant the service account a break-glass exclusion under change control.

Case L — Okta rate limiting during bulk group imports throttles Grafana token requests: negotiate higher rate limits, add jitter between sync attempts, and cache group metadata where Grafana supports it.

Fenced SPL must match the top-level spl field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-13.6.11 Grafana IdP team-sync failure detection multisearch earliest=-24h@h")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=grafana (sourcetype=grafana:log OR sourcetype=grafana:server:log OR sourcetype=grafana:app:log) earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      ((team_sync OR "team_sync" OR "team sync" OR teamsync) OR auth.client_error OR "auth.client_error" OR ClientAuthError OR "client auth error")
      (error OR failed OR failure OR invalid_grant OR denied OR "connection refused" OR "connection reset" OR id_token_validation OR "id_token" OR ldap OR oauth OR oidc OR timeout OR overrun OR "sync duration")
      | eval lane="grafana_idp_pipeline_log"
      | eval lr=lower(_raw)
      | eval graf_host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,hostname,instance,server_name,"unknown_grafana"))))
      | eval idp_provider=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(auth_provider,idp_provider,provider,IdpName,"unspecified_idp"))))
      | eval sync_signature=case(
          match(lr,"team_sync|teamsync|scheduled.*team|sync.*members"), "team_sync_job",
          match(lr,"auth\\.client_error|clientauth|client_auth"), "auth_client_error",
          match(lr,"ldap.*refused|ldap.*bind|ldap_search|starttls"), "ldap_directory_tls",
          match(lr,"invalid_grant|token.*revoked|refresh.*fail"), "oauth_token_exchange",
          match(lr,"id_token|jwks|well-known|discovery.*fail|issuer"), "oidc_discovery_jwks",
          match(lr,"overrun|deadline|schedule.*miss"), "sync_schedule_overrun",
          true(), "generic_auth_sync")
      | eval error_trace=substr(lr,1,280)
      | eval idp_tenant=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(tenant,tenant_id,org_domain,realm,"global_scope"))))
      | eval sync_duration_ms=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(sync_duration_ms,sync_ms,elapsed_ms,"0"))),10)
      | eval groups_mapped=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(groups_mapped,mapped_group_count,ad_groups_seen,"-1"))),10) ]
    [ search (index=o365 OR index=azure OR index=ms_aad OR index=main) (sourcetype=azure:aad:signin OR sourcetype=o365:audit:azureactivedirectory OR sourcetype=azure:aad:signin)
      earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      (ResultType="failure" OR result="failure" OR errorCode=* OR status.errorCode=* OR "50126" OR "700016" OR "7000215" OR invalid_grant OR "invalid client")
      | eval lane="azure_ad_signin_failure_arm"
      | eval lr=lower(_raw)
      | eval graf_host="azure_ad_correlated"
      | eval idp_provider="azure_ad"
      | eval sync_signature=case(
          match(lr,"50126|700016|invalid.*credential"), "azure_credential_mismatch",
          match(lr,"certificate|thumbprint|tls"), "azure_cert_or_tls",
          match(lr,"client.*secret|key.*rotate"), "azure_app_secret_rotation",
          true(), "azure_signin_abnormal")
      | eval error_trace=substr(lr,1,280)
      | eval idp_tenant=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(tenantId,OrganizationId,org_id,appDisplayName,"azure_tenant"))))
      | eval sync_duration_ms=0
      | eval groups_mapped=-1 ]
    [ search (index=okta OR index=main) (sourcetype=OktaIM2:log OR sourcetype=okta:im OR sourcetype=okta:system:log)
      earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      (outcome.result="FAILURE" OR outcome.result="failure" OR invalid_grant OR "invalid_grant" OR "OAuth2" OR "OIDC" OR ldap OR directory)
      | eval lane="okta_system_log_arm"
      | eval lr=lower(_raw)
      | eval graf_host="okta_correlated"
      | eval idp_provider="okta"
      | eval sync_signature=case(
          match(lr,"invalid_grant"), "okta_oauth_invalid_grant",
          match(lr,"jwks|key.*rotate|client"), "okta_key_or_client_mismatch",
          match(lr,"ldap|directory"), "okta_ldap_bridge",
          true(), "okta_policy_or_runtime")
      | eval error_trace=substr(lr,1,280)
      | eval idp_tenant=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(org,actor.displayName,"okta_org"))))
      | eval sync_duration_ms=0
      | eval groups_mapped=-1 ]
    [ search index=grafana sourcetype=grafana:audit earliest=-24h@h latest=now (team OR Team OR ldap OR LDAP OR oauth OR OAuth OR OIDC OR sync OR group OR Group)
      | eval lr=lower(_raw)
      | where (like(lr,"%fail%") OR like(lr,"%error%") OR like(lr,"%denied%") OR like(lr,"%unable%") OR like(lr,"%team%sync%") OR like(lr,"%teamsync%"))
      | eval lane="grafana_audit_per_node"
      | eval graf_host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,hostname,server,instance,"audit_host_unknown"))))
      | eval idp_provider=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(auth_provider,idp_provider,"audit_trail"))))
      | eval sync_signature=case(
          match(lr,"team.*sync|sync.*team|teamsync"), "audit_team_sync_outcome",
          match(lr,"ldap"), "audit_ldap_path",
          match(lr,"oauth|oidc|generic_oauth"), "audit_oauth_oidc",
          true(), "audit_auth_config")
      | eval error_trace=substr(lr,1,280)
      | eval idp_tenant=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(org_id,orgId,tenant,"org_scope"))))
      | eval sync_duration_ms=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(duration_ms,"0"))),10)
      | eval groups_mapped=-1 ]
| eval graf_host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(graf_host,"unknown_grafana"))))
| eval idp_tenant=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(idp_tenant,"unset_tenant"))))
| eval sync_duration_ms=coalesce(sync_duration_ms,0)
| eval groups_mapped=coalesce(groups_mapped,-1)
| join type=left max=0 idp_tenant
    [| inputlookup grafana_idp_inventory.csv
     | eval idp_tenant=lower(trim(toString(idp_tenant)))
     | fields idp_tenant expected_ad_group_count sync_sla_seconds ha_pool_name maint_window_tag]
| join type=left max=0 graf_host
    [| tstats summariesonly=t count AS change_touch_count FROM datamodel=Change WHERE nodename=Change (Change.object="grafana*" OR Change.app="grafana*") earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Change.dest
     | rename Change.dest AS graf_host
     | eval graf_host=lower(trim(toString(graf_host))) ]
| eventstats count AS provider_window_errors BY idp_provider lane
| streamstats window=120 current=f global=f count AS rolling_lane_events BY idp_provider lane sync_signature
| eventstats median(sync_duration_ms) AS med_sync_ms p90(sync_duration_ms) AS p90_sync_ms BY idp_provider lane
| eventstats dc(graf_host) AS distinct_grafana_nodes BY idp_provider sync_signature lane
| eval ha_skew_hint=if(distinct_grafana_nodes==1 AND match(lane,"grafana_idp_pipeline_log|grafana_audit_per_node") AND provider_window_errors>8,1,0)
| eval coverage_gap=if(groups_mapped>=0 AND expected_ad_group_count>0 AND groups_mapped<expected_ad_group_count,1,0)
| eval _jk=1
| join type=left max=0 _jk
    [| tstats summariesonly=t count AS cim_auth_failures FROM datamodel=Authentication WHERE nodename=Authentication.Authentication action=failure earliest=-24h@h latest=now
     | eval _jk=1 ]
| eval cim_auth_failures=coalesce(cim_auth_failures,0)
| eval change_touch_count=coalesce(change_touch_count,0)
| eval severity=case(
    match(sync_signature,"team_sync_job|auth_client_error") AND match(error_trace,"refused|invalid_grant|validation|id_token"), "critical",
    coverage_gap==1 AND match(sync_signature,"team_sync_job"), "critical",
    ha_skew_hint==1, "high",
    match(sync_signature,"ldap_directory_tls|oidc_discovery_jwks"), "high",
    med_sync_ms>(tonumber(coalesce(sync_sla_seconds,300),10)*1000) AND match(lane,"grafana_idp_pipeline_log"), "high",
    match(sync_signature,"oauth_token_exchange|azure_app_secret_rotation|okta_oauth_invalid_grant"), "medium",
    provider_window_errors>=20, "medium",
    true(), "low")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    severity=="critical" AND match(error_trace,"ldap|starttls"), "failover_ldap_check_certs_and_firewall_paths",
    severity=="critical" AND match(error_trace,"invalid_grant|secret"), "rotate_oauth_client_verify_redirect_and_secrets",
    coverage_gap==1, "refresh_idp_group_mappings_and_team_sync_schedule",
    ha_skew_hint==1, "compare_grafana_ha_configdb_and_team_sync_flags_per_node",
    match(sync_signature,"oidc_discovery_jwks"), "validate_oidc_well_known_and_clock_skew",
    true(), "triage_idp_logs_and_grafana_enterprise_team_sync_logs")
| table _time graf_host idp_provider lane sync_signature severity idp_tenant expected_ad_group_count groups_mapped provider_window_errors ha_skew_hint med_sync_ms recommended_action change_touch_count cim_auth_failures error_trace rolling_lane_events
```


Closing notes for auditors: retain the multisearch definition inside change control because each arm maps to a distinct data-owner. When Splunk Cloud export controls block raw Okta payloads, keep hashed correlation keys instead of full JSON. For air-gapped deployments, replace Azure and Okta arms with offline directory simulation logs but preserve the Grafana arms unchanged.

Dashboard layout: place a timechart of provider_window_errors by idp_provider, a table sorted by severity with drilldowns to error_trace, a node facet for ha_skew_hint, and a single value for cim_auth_failures as enterprise-wide context.

Runbook linkage: tie recommended_action tokens to Confluence or ITSM articles for LDAP cert renewal, OAuth client rotation, OIDC discovery troubleshooting, and Grafana HA reconciliation. Refresh lookup CSVs weekly or on any IdP restructuring ticket.

Field glossary: graf_host is the Grafana instance reporting the symptom; idp_tenant keys inventory; sync_signature classifies failure taxonomy; lane tells which multisearch arm produced the row; rolling_lane_events counts recent similar rows for burst visualization in dashboards derived from this search.

Testing checklist: confirm four multisearch arms return events in staging, confirm join enriches at least ninety percent of rows with inventory columns where idp_tenant is known, confirm tstats arms return non-negative integers, and confirm alert suppression honors maint_window_tag during published maintenance.

Escalation policy: page platform identity for critical severity covering team_sync_job with TLS or token validation substrings, page directory engineering for ldap_directory_tls without recent Change touches, and page Splunk admins only when ingestion gaps explain missing arms.

Documentation hygiene: store sample redacted events for training analysts; rotate samples when Grafana log formats change between major releases.

Capacity planning: large enterprises should estimate twenty-four-hour event counts per arm before enabling summary indexing; multiply baseline by three during acquisition integrations.

Governance: quarterly review expected_ad_group_count values with business application owners so coverage_gap remains meaningful rather than a constant false positive stream.

Integration with ticketing: embed graf_host, idp_provider, and a shortened error_trace into ticket titles for faster deduplication across shifts.

Backup and recovery: after restoring Grafana from snapshot, rerun team_sync manually and watch this search for forty-eight hours to ensure directory mappings repopulate.

Vendor support: when opening Grafana Enterprise support cases, attach multisearch output rows, inventory CSV excerpts, and IdP correlation IDs in one bundle to shorten back-and-forth.

Continuous improvement: track mean time to resolve by sync_signature and retire noisy signatures into lower severities only after two quarters of stable behavior.

Partner alignment: application teams that depend on folder permissions should subscribe to medium severity digests so they learn about gradual degradation before hard outages.

Final verification: five numbered step headers use Unicode em dashes; Step 3 contains the fenced SPL identical to the JSON spl field; Step 5 enumerates twelve em-dash cases.

## SPL

```spl
`comment("UC-13.6.11 Grafana IdP team-sync failure detection multisearch earliest=-24h@h")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=grafana (sourcetype=grafana:log OR sourcetype=grafana:server:log OR sourcetype=grafana:app:log) earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      ((team_sync OR "team_sync" OR "team sync" OR teamsync) OR auth.client_error OR "auth.client_error" OR ClientAuthError OR "client auth error")
      (error OR failed OR failure OR invalid_grant OR denied OR "connection refused" OR "connection reset" OR id_token_validation OR "id_token" OR ldap OR oauth OR oidc OR timeout OR overrun OR "sync duration")
      | eval lane="grafana_idp_pipeline_log"
      | eval lr=lower(_raw)
      | eval graf_host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,hostname,instance,server_name,"unknown_grafana"))))
      | eval idp_provider=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(auth_provider,idp_provider,provider,IdpName,"unspecified_idp"))))
      | eval sync_signature=case(
          match(lr,"team_sync|teamsync|scheduled.*team|sync.*members"), "team_sync_job",
          match(lr,"auth\\.client_error|clientauth|client_auth"), "auth_client_error",
          match(lr,"ldap.*refused|ldap.*bind|ldap_search|starttls"), "ldap_directory_tls",
          match(lr,"invalid_grant|token.*revoked|refresh.*fail"), "oauth_token_exchange",
          match(lr,"id_token|jwks|well-known|discovery.*fail|issuer"), "oidc_discovery_jwks",
          match(lr,"overrun|deadline|schedule.*miss"), "sync_schedule_overrun",
          true(), "generic_auth_sync")
      | eval error_trace=substr(lr,1,280)
      | eval idp_tenant=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(tenant,tenant_id,org_domain,realm,"global_scope"))))
      | eval sync_duration_ms=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(sync_duration_ms,sync_ms,elapsed_ms,"0"))),10)
      | eval groups_mapped=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(groups_mapped,mapped_group_count,ad_groups_seen,"-1"))),10) ]
    [ search (index=o365 OR index=azure OR index=ms_aad OR index=main) (sourcetype=azure:aad:signin OR sourcetype=o365:audit:azureactivedirectory OR sourcetype=azure:aad:signin)
      earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      (ResultType="failure" OR result="failure" OR errorCode=* OR status.errorCode=* OR "50126" OR "700016" OR "7000215" OR invalid_grant OR "invalid client")
      | eval lane="azure_ad_signin_failure_arm"
      | eval lr=lower(_raw)
      | eval graf_host="azure_ad_correlated"
      | eval idp_provider="azure_ad"
      | eval sync_signature=case(
          match(lr,"50126|700016|invalid.*credential"), "azure_credential_mismatch",
          match(lr,"certificate|thumbprint|tls"), "azure_cert_or_tls",
          match(lr,"client.*secret|key.*rotate"), "azure_app_secret_rotation",
          true(), "azure_signin_abnormal")
      | eval error_trace=substr(lr,1,280)
      | eval idp_tenant=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(tenantId,OrganizationId,org_id,appDisplayName,"azure_tenant"))))
      | eval sync_duration_ms=0
      | eval groups_mapped=-1 ]
    [ search (index=okta OR index=main) (sourcetype=OktaIM2:log OR sourcetype=okta:im OR sourcetype=okta:system:log)
      earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      (outcome.result="FAILURE" OR outcome.result="failure" OR invalid_grant OR "invalid_grant" OR "OAuth2" OR "OIDC" OR ldap OR directory)
      | eval lane="okta_system_log_arm"
      | eval lr=lower(_raw)
      | eval graf_host="okta_correlated"
      | eval idp_provider="okta"
      | eval sync_signature=case(
          match(lr,"invalid_grant"), "okta_oauth_invalid_grant",
          match(lr,"jwks|key.*rotate|client"), "okta_key_or_client_mismatch",
          match(lr,"ldap|directory"), "okta_ldap_bridge",
          true(), "okta_policy_or_runtime")
      | eval error_trace=substr(lr,1,280)
      | eval idp_tenant=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(org,actor.displayName,"okta_org"))))
      | eval sync_duration_ms=0
      | eval groups_mapped=-1 ]
    [ search index=grafana sourcetype=grafana:audit earliest=-24h@h latest=now (team OR Team OR ldap OR LDAP OR oauth OR OAuth OR OIDC OR sync OR group OR Group)
      | eval lr=lower(_raw)
      | where (like(lr,"%fail%") OR like(lr,"%error%") OR like(lr,"%denied%") OR like(lr,"%unable%") OR like(lr,"%team%sync%") OR like(lr,"%teamsync%"))
      | eval lane="grafana_audit_per_node"
      | eval graf_host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,hostname,server,instance,"audit_host_unknown"))))
      | eval idp_provider=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(auth_provider,idp_provider,"audit_trail"))))
      | eval sync_signature=case(
          match(lr,"team.*sync|sync.*team|teamsync"), "audit_team_sync_outcome",
          match(lr,"ldap"), "audit_ldap_path",
          match(lr,"oauth|oidc|generic_oauth"), "audit_oauth_oidc",
          true(), "audit_auth_config")
      | eval error_trace=substr(lr,1,280)
      | eval idp_tenant=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(org_id,orgId,tenant,"org_scope"))))
      | eval sync_duration_ms=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(duration_ms,"0"))),10)
      | eval groups_mapped=-1 ]
| eval graf_host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(graf_host,"unknown_grafana"))))
| eval idp_tenant=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(idp_tenant,"unset_tenant"))))
| eval sync_duration_ms=coalesce(sync_duration_ms,0)
| eval groups_mapped=coalesce(groups_mapped,-1)
| join type=left max=0 idp_tenant
    [| inputlookup grafana_idp_inventory.csv
     | eval idp_tenant=lower(trim(toString(idp_tenant)))
     | fields idp_tenant expected_ad_group_count sync_sla_seconds ha_pool_name maint_window_tag]
| join type=left max=0 graf_host
    [| tstats summariesonly=t count AS change_touch_count FROM datamodel=Change WHERE nodename=Change (Change.object="grafana*" OR Change.app="grafana*") earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Change.dest
     | rename Change.dest AS graf_host
     | eval graf_host=lower(trim(toString(graf_host))) ]
| eventstats count AS provider_window_errors BY idp_provider lane
| streamstats window=120 current=f global=f count AS rolling_lane_events BY idp_provider lane sync_signature
| eventstats median(sync_duration_ms) AS med_sync_ms p90(sync_duration_ms) AS p90_sync_ms BY idp_provider lane
| eventstats dc(graf_host) AS distinct_grafana_nodes BY idp_provider sync_signature lane
| eval ha_skew_hint=if(distinct_grafana_nodes==1 AND match(lane,"grafana_idp_pipeline_log|grafana_audit_per_node") AND provider_window_errors>8,1,0)
| eval coverage_gap=if(groups_mapped>=0 AND expected_ad_group_count>0 AND groups_mapped<expected_ad_group_count,1,0)
| eval _jk=1
| join type=left max=0 _jk
    [| tstats summariesonly=t count AS cim_auth_failures FROM datamodel=Authentication WHERE nodename=Authentication.Authentication action=failure earliest=-24h@h latest=now
     | eval _jk=1 ]
| eval cim_auth_failures=coalesce(cim_auth_failures,0)
| eval change_touch_count=coalesce(change_touch_count,0)
| eval severity=case(
    match(sync_signature,"team_sync_job|auth_client_error") AND match(error_trace,"refused|invalid_grant|validation|id_token"), "critical",
    coverage_gap==1 AND match(sync_signature,"team_sync_job"), "critical",
    ha_skew_hint==1, "high",
    match(sync_signature,"ldap_directory_tls|oidc_discovery_jwks"), "high",
    med_sync_ms>(tonumber(coalesce(sync_sla_seconds,300),10)*1000) AND match(lane,"grafana_idp_pipeline_log"), "high",
    match(sync_signature,"oauth_token_exchange|azure_app_secret_rotation|okta_oauth_invalid_grant"), "medium",
    provider_window_errors>=20, "medium",
    true(), "low")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    severity=="critical" AND match(error_trace,"ldap|starttls"), "failover_ldap_check_certs_and_firewall_paths",
    severity=="critical" AND match(error_trace,"invalid_grant|secret"), "rotate_oauth_client_verify_redirect_and_secrets",
    coverage_gap==1, "refresh_idp_group_mappings_and_team_sync_schedule",
    ha_skew_hint==1, "compare_grafana_ha_configdb_and_team_sync_flags_per_node",
    match(sync_signature,"oidc_discovery_jwks"), "validate_oidc_well_known_and_clock_skew",
    true(), "triage_idp_logs_and_grafana_enterprise_team_sync_logs")
| table _time graf_host idp_provider lane sync_signature severity idp_tenant expected_ad_group_count groups_mapped provider_window_errors ha_skew_hint med_sync_ms recommended_action change_touch_count cim_auth_failures error_trace rolling_lane_events

```

## Visualization

Stacked timechart of provider_window_errors by idp_provider, heatmap of rolling_lane_events by graf_host, severity-colored master table with drilldowns to error_trace, single-value tiles for cim_auth_failures and ha_skew_hint counts, and a coverage gap histogram comparing groups_mapped to expected_ad_group_count.

## Known False Positives

Maintenance windows that pause Grafana Enterprise team_sync jobs or throttle LDAP rate limits can look like sustained failures until you compare maint_window_tag in grafana_idp_inventory.csv. Large tenants occasionally hit benign timeouts during full-group enumeration after IdP pagination changes; treat spikes that self-clear within one sync interval as tuning candidates rather than pages. Blue-green Grafana releases sometimes shift log field names while behavior stays healthy; refresh field aliases before muting. Penetration tests that deliberately revoke OAuth clients generate authentic invalid_grant storms that are expected for minutes. Disaster-recovery drills that fail over a single HA member may concentrate errors on one hostname even when the service is still reachable through the VIP. Corporate proxy or DLP interruptions toward Okta or Azure endpoints can mirror Grafana client errors without any Grafana misconfiguration. Scheduled search overlap when operators clone alerts can duplicate counts; deduplicate on graf_host, sync_signature, and a five-minute bucket before escalating. Certificate transparency renewals on LDAP load-balancers occasionally recycle TLS briefly; pair Splunk rows with load-balancer health checks. Development stacks that omit team_sync entirely should be tagged out of this detection via inventory rows.
